findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find all methods in FileSecurityUtils
 * @description Examine all methods in FileSecurityUtils for path traversal vulnerabilities
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/file-security-utils-methods
 */
import java

from Method m
where m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils")
select m, "Method: " + m.getName() + " - " + m.getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName()
findings.hypothesis: Examine all methods in FileSecurityUtils to understand path validation logic

message.text: Method: normalize - io.whitesource.cure.FileSecurityUtils
Method: isFileOutsideDir - io.whitesource.cure.FileSecurityUtils

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Examine FileSecurityUtils methods in detail
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/file-security-utils-detail
 */
import java

from Method m, Class c
where c.hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils")
  and m.getDeclaringType() = c
select m, "Method: " + m.getName() + " | Return type: " + m.getReturnType() + " | Params: " + count(m.getAParameter()) + " | Body lines: " + m.getNumberOfLinesOfCode()
findings.hypothesis: Understand the FileSecurityUtils methods to identify potential path traversal vulnerabilities

message.text: Method: normalize | Return type: String | Params: 1 | Body lines: 6
Method: isFileOutsideDir | Return type: boolean | Params: 2 | Body lines: 6

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Examine all method calls within FileSecurityUtils
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/file-security-utils-calls
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils")
select mc, "Call: " + mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Understand what methods are called within FileSecurityUtils to find missing canonicalization

message.text: Call: String.startsWith at line 25
Call: File.getCanonicalPath at line 25
Call: File.getCanonicalPath at line 25
Call: FilenameUtils.normalize at line 38

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Detailed analysis of FileSecurityUtils isFileOutsideDir and normalize methods
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/filesecutils-detailed-analysis
 */
import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsolutePath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("normalize") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath")
  )
select mc, "Method " + m.getName() + " calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " on qualifier type: " + mc.getQualifier().getType()
findings.hypothesis: Understand full chain of calls in isFileOutsideDir and normalize methods to identify path traversal bypass

message.text: Method isFileOutsideDir calls startsWith at line 25 on qualifier type: String
Method isFileOutsideDir calls getCanonicalPath at line 25 on qualifier type: File
Method isFileOutsideDir calls getCanonicalPath at line 25 on qualifier type: File
Method normalize calls normalize at line 38 on qualifier type: FilenameUtils

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Check what FilenameUtils.normalize argument is in normalize method
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/filenameutils-normalize-arg
 */
import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc, Expr arg
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  mc.getMethod().hasName("normalize") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.commons.io", "FilenameUtils") and
  arg = mc.getArgument(0)
select mc, "FilenameUtils.normalize call in method " + m.getName() + " with arg: " + arg + " of type " + arg.getType()
findings.hypothesis: Check if FilenameUtils.normalize gets a raw user path vs already-processed path

message.text: FilenameUtils.normalize call in method normalize with arg: filePath of type String

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Check startsWith path containment with string comparison
 * @description String.startsWith for path containment can be bypassed (CWE-22)
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/startswith-path-check
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType() instanceof TypeString and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils")
select mc, "String.startsWith used for path containment check in " + m.getName() + 
  " - can be bypassed when directory path lacks trailing separator (e.g., /safe matches /safe-evil)"
findings.hypothesis: String.startsWith for path containment check can be bypassed when the base directory path doesn't end with a separator - e.g., /safe would match /safe-evil/file

message.text: String.startsWith used for path containment check in isFileOutsideDir - can be bypassed when directory path lacks trailing separator (e.g., /safe matches /safe-evil)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Analyze startsWith argument in isFileOutsideDir - check for separator
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/startswith-separator-check
 */
import java

from Method m, MethodCall startsWith
where
  m.hasName("isFileOutsideDir") and
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils") and
  startsWith.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  startsWith.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  startsWith.getMethod().getDeclaringType() instanceof TypeString
select startsWith, "startsWith call - qualifier: " + startsWith.getQualifier().toString() + 
  " | arg0: " + startsWith.getArgument(0).toString()
findings.hypothesis: Check if the startsWith argument in isFileOutsideDir includes a separator to prevent /safe matching /safe-evil

 * @description Check if directory path in startsWith check has separator appended
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning

 */
import java

from Method m, MethodCall startsWith, Expr arg
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils") and
  m.hasName("isFileOutsideDir") and

 */
import java

from Method m, MethodCall startsWith, Expr arg0
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils") and
  m.hasName("isFileOutsideDir") and

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Path traversal via startsWith without separator and FilenameUtils.normalize bypass
 * @description Identifies path containment checks using String.startsWith without a trailing separator,
 *              which can be bypassed (e.g., /safe-evil passes /safe check). Also identifies
 *              FilenameUtils.normalize which can return null for paths with too many ../ or handle
 *              file: protocol prefixes in an unexpected way.
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/path-traversal-startswith
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType() instanceof TypeString and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.whitesource.cure", "FileSecurityUtils") and
  // The argument to startsWith is a method call (getCanonicalPath), not a concatenation with separator
  mc.getArgument(0) instanceof MethodCall and
  not mc.getArgument(0) instanceof AddExpr
select mc, "Path containment check uses String.startsWith() without appending a file separator. " +
  "A directory named '/safe' would incorrectly allow access to '/safe-evil', enabling path traversal (CWE-022)."
findings.hypothesis: String.startsWith used for path containment check without trailing separator - allows /safe to match /safe-evil

message.text: Path containment check uses String.startsWith() without appending a file separator. A directory named '/safe' would incorrectly allow access to '/safe-evil', enabling path traversal (CWE-022).
